checks.a_analysis: The output JSON contains only text found in the source document. No fabricated sections, headers, or body text were identified. The headings and paragraphs match the instructional text provided in the source PDF across all three pages.

There are no phone numbers, email addresses, or specific URLs in the source document or the output JSON to compare. The mentions of the 'Secure Internet Portal' refer to the system name, not a fabricated external link.

All content from the three-page source document is accounted for in the output JSON. Page 1 (initial steps), Page 2 (entering key and portal acceptance), and Page 3 (success and final verification) are all present and sequentially correct.
The source document contains no tables. This check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The core instructional text for every step is preserved in the JSON output. Each paragraph in the JSON maps directly to a line of instruction in the source PDF without substantial omission.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content in the JSON is a faithful representation of the source. Minor differences in punctuation or whitespace are within acceptable limits for a conversion. Meaning is consistently preserved throughout.

checks.a_analysis: A comprehensive review of the output document confirms that no content has been duplicated. Each paragraph and image from the three-page source document appears exactly once in the JSON output, maintaining strict adherence to the original content frequency.

The heading hierarchy is correctly represented. The document title is established as the primary section title. The subsequent instructions, which are not visually distinctive as headers in the source, are appropriately categorized as paragraphs. There are no instances of skipped heading levels.
The source document does not contain any tables. The output correctly reflects this by having no table elements.

The reading order in the output is identical to the source document. The sequence follows the logical progression of instructional text followed by a relevant screenshot, preserving the pedagogical flow across the page boundaries of the original PDF.

No lists were identified in the source document. The instructions are presented as discrete paragraphs, and this structure is faithfully preserved in the output without the introduction of list markers or nesting.
There are no clickable links or visible URLs in the source document. The output correctly contains no link elements, ensuring no broken or fabricated links were injected during conversion.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the converted document matches the source PDF. The document follows a consistent pattern of an instructional text block followed by a corresponding image or placeholder. The sequential flow of the user guide is preserved, allowing a user to find information in the same relative order as the original.
There are no tables present in the source PDF or the HTML output.

Most of the instructional images present in the source PDF are missing in the rendered HTML output. While the first two images are present, seven subsequent meaningful images (screenshots of the WiFi selection list, connection dialogs, security key entry, splash page, and success screens) are replaced by green placeholder boxes. Since these images are essential for the user to follow the guide visually, this is a major defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 9 meaningful screenshots/images across 3 pages (e.g., WiFi list on page 1, security key entry on page 2, login successful screen on page 3).
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, 7 out of the 9 images are missing and represented by green rectangular blocks.
checks.b_evidence_location: Starting from the instruction 'Select NCGov-Guest' through to the end of the document.

checks.a_analysis: No visual duplication of text blocks or images was found in the HTML output.
